main_turf.bias_notes: Counter-Clockwise. The new Gifford Hill layout is a massive, sprawling track that is a generous 30 metres wide. It features a beautifully cambered, sweeping home turn and a punishing 450m home straight. It is universally regarded as one of the fairest tracks in South Australia. The long straight and wide expanse virtually eliminate early speed bias, giving every horse (especially deep closers) a legitimate chance.
main_turf.post_bias: Extremely fair. Because the track is 30 metres wide and the runs into the first turns are generally generous, wide barriers (outside draws) are far less of a penalty here than at Morphettville or tighter SA provincial tracks. Horses have ample time to find cover and balance up.

handicapping_angles.the_gifford_hill_swoop: Heavily downgrade one-dimensional front-runners ('Need-the-Lead' types) who rely on tight turns to steal races. The massive 450m straight will ruthlessly expose any horse lacking genuine stamina. Aggressively upgrade 'Swoopers' (closers) with a strong late turn of foot.
handicapping_angles.metropolitan_class_alternate: Murray Bridge frequently hosts premium Saturday Metro meetings when Morphettville is resting. Always treat Saturday Murray Bridge form with the exact same respect (Class Rating) as Morphettville form, and upgrade horses stepping back to midweek country/provincial grades from this track.
handicapping_angles.free_draining_profile: The Gifford Hill track was engineered from scratch with a state-of-the-art, free-draining sand profile. It handles winter rain significantly better than most South Australian tracks. Do not over-penalize form based on minor rain, and wait for genuinely heavy, sustained downpours before applying strict 'mudlark' wet-track logic.
